message:
fix(restore): sanitize CR/LF from restored command to close ft-kegvt command-injection defense-in-depth

ft-kegvt

restore_process.rs launch_agent_legacy (line 625) and launch_agent_cx
(line 648) both type the restored `command` string into the pane's
interactive shell via `wezterm.send_text(pane_id, format!("{command}\r"))`.
The shell — not argv-isolated exec — interprets whatever characters
land in the pane, so any CR/LF in the command sneaks additional lines
into the shell input buffer. `cwd` was already passing through
`shell_escape`, but `command` was unsanitized; the asymmetry was the
defense-in-depth gap filed in ft-kegvt.

`RestoredPaneState.command` is loaded from the `mux_pane_state`
SQLite table. The attacker-reachable paths — diag-bundle import,
snapshot restore from an untrusted source, targeted DB tamper — all
flow through `launch_agent_{legacy,cx}` on `ft restart --launch-agents`.

## Fix

Added `sanitize_restored_command(&str) -> Result<&str, String>` next
to `shell_escape` in the helpers section. Rejects:
- `\r` / `\n` — primary CR/LF injection vector.
- `\x1b` (ESC) — blocks ANSI/OSC re-injection that could re-arm
  Kitty keyboard protocol or trigger OSC handlers mid-command.
- Other C0 controls (`0x00..=0x1f` except `\t`) — conservative
  catch-all; no legitimate restored agent command contains them.

Both launch_agent variants now gate `command` through this
sanitizer before constructing the `format!("{safe_command}\r")`
payload. On rejection, the restore helper returns `Err(...)` with
a stringly-typed message including the byte offset and `ft-kegvt`
tag for future triage.

## Tests (8 new)

Inline next to `shell_escape_spaces`:

- `sanitize_restored_command_passes_legitimate_input` — 6 canonical
  agent restart commands (claude-code variants, `codex resume <uuid>`,
  gemini, env-prefixed, TAB-indented) must all roundtrip unchanged.
- `sanitize_restored_command_rejects_newline_injection` — the exact
  attack payload `claude-code\ninnocent_payload\r` from the bead's
  fix directive MUST be rejected; assertion pins both the "CR/LF"
  reason and the ft-kegvt tag.
- `sanitize_restored_command_rejects_bare_carriage_return` /
  `_rejects_bare_newline` — individual CR and LF bytes reject.
- `sanitize_restored_command_rejects_ansi_escape` — `\x1b]0;pwned\x07`
  (OSC title-set) rejects at the ESC byte.
- `sanitize_restored_command_rejects_c0_control` — BEL (0x07) and
  NUL (0x00) reject under the generic C0 branch.
- `sanitize_restored_command_preserves_utf8` — multi-byte UTF-8
  (Cyrillic + CJK) passes; sanitizer only rejects the explicit
  control set.
- `sanitize_restored_command_rejects_lf_then_cr` — byte-offset is
  reported correctly for the FIRST offending byte (fail-fast).

## Non-changes

- `shell_escape` unchanged (already defensive).
- `send_text` API surface unchanged; sanitization is a call-site
  concern, not a protocol-layer concern.
- Public `ProcessLauncher` API unchanged.

## Out of scope

- Longer-term: routing relaunch through `wezterm cli spawn --` with
  argv-isolated arguments would eliminate the interactive-shell
  path entirely. The bead documents this as a follow-up; this fix
  is the defense-in-depth closure, not the structural rewrite.

## Related

- ft-security-mcp-path-traversal (30988e53) — same class of DID gap
  across the MCP tool surface.
- ft-xv561 (070117a0) — pattern-engine ReDoS via user packs.
